<<Flask CLI and App Factory Integration>>

Running a Flask app with the app factory pattern allows for flexible configuration. When using CLI commands, you can define them with `@app.cli.command`. However, using a different `create_app` function for CLI commands can be tricky. One approach is to use environment variables to differentiate between server and CLI execution. This method requires setting `FLASK_MY_VARIABLE=x` each time you run the CLI command. By reading this variable, you can adjust the behavior of your app. The environment variable method is reliable but may not be ideal for all use cases. 

* * *

Using environment variables is a common practice in Flask applications. It allows you to control the app's behavior based on the environment. However, it requires users to remember to set the variable each time they run the CLI command. This can be inconvenient for frequent use. Instead, you can pass parameters directly to the CLI command. This approach simplifies the process and avoids the need for environment variables. The parameter can be used to determine whether the app was invoked as a CLI command or as a server. This method is more user-friendly and reduces the risk of errors. By using parameters, you can make your CLI commands more versatile and easier to use.

* * *
